text:
REGIME — Risk-on rally, tech-led, breadth lagging, liquidity tightening.

INDICES — VIX 17.9, down 0.14% on the day.[1] QQQ +0.48%, RSP +0.46%, IJR +0.33%, SPY +0.25%, IWM +0.21%, DIA +0.19%.[1] Large-cap outpacing small-cap despite breadth underperformance: RSP up 4.15% over 20d versus SPY up 6.37%, a 2.22pp gap.[1] Oil rallied 2.65% to lead cross-assets as Middle East ceasefire extension signals risk premium relief.[1]

SECTORS — XLE +1.47%, XLC +1.25%, XLK +1.14%, XLRE +0.92%, XLU +0.72%, XLB +0.72%, XLP +0.46%, XLF -0.27%, XLY -0.47%, XLI -0.50%, XLV -0.79%.[1] Rare earths REMX +5.76% and telecom IYZ +4.39% led; defense ITA -2.12% and cannabis MSOS -3.14% lagged.[1]

New Metals 7 +4.02%, Freight 7 +2.94%, Semi 7 +2.40%, Cloud 7 +2.07%, Software 7 +1.14%, Energy 7 +1.08%, Insurance 7 +0.87%, Power 7 +0.71%, Old Staples 7 +0.34%, Toll Road 7 +0.17%, Mag 7 +0.14%, Old Metals 7 +0.13%, Buildout 7 -0.02%, New Staples 7 -0.16%, Housing 7 -0.47%, Health 7 -0.52%, Bank 7 -0.74%, Industrial 7 -0.83%, Defense 7 -1.05%, Fab 7 -1.56%, Medtech 7 -2.53%.[1] ALB +16.31% strongest in New Metals 7; Medtech 7 weakness led by unnamed component at -2.53%.[1]

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC April 29, 2026.[1] CME probabilities: hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%.[1] Hawk/neutral/dove tally: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves.[1] USD liquidity stress elevated at 66/100; commercial paper-Treasury bill spread 0.20% ranks 95th percentile, most-stressed component.[1] TED spread 0.09% contained; ON-RRP $0.2B minimal.[1]

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 62.4/100 (Greed).[1] Top movers: ALB +16.31% (New Metals 7), JBHT +6.31% (Freight 7), IYZ +4.39% (Communications).[1] 20d momentum: XLK best sector +10.33%, XLV worst -0.05%; New Metals 7 best +17.24%, Defense 7 worst -3.84%.[1] AI and Q1 bank earnings drove Technology +2.08% and Consumer Discretionary strength; Materials +0.34% cyclical tailwind aligned with two-day rally above S&P 500 7,000.[1]

WATCH — ALB: +16.31%, vol 2.8x avg, lithium rally on ceasefire-driven risk-on and strong rare-earth complex momentum; watch for reversal if Middle East tensions resurface. IYZ: +4.39%, vol 3.9x avg, telecom breakout concurrent with AI infrastructure demand thesis; test whether sector sustains above recent resistance. TSM: -3.13%, vol 2.0x avg, semiconductor divergence despite Semi 7 +2.40%; cross-asset signal suggests
